title: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest
source: CDLI tablet photograph (P397688) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
description: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Journey to the Cedar Forest; dreams of Gilgamesh; approach to Humbaba's domain. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P397688). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).
introduction:
This entry presents Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest of the Epic of Gilgamesh — the oldest major work of literature, composed in Akkadian cuneiform around 2100-1200 BCE.

  1. Tablet photograph: Cuneiform tablet photo from CDLI (artifact P397688), showing the actual clay tablet with the wedge-shaped script.
  2. Original text: Akkadian transliteration from Langdon's scholarly edition (public domain).
  3. English translation: Langdon's English rendering (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

translation.original_ref: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest, section 1
translation.original_text:
Dungi, king of Ur, liturgy to, 136.

_dupsakku_, trencher basket, 216, 17.

Duranki, epithet for Nippur, 122, 18; 180, 11.
translation.text:
Dungi, king of Ur, liturgy to, 136.

_dupsakku_, trencher basket, 216, 17.

Duranki, epithet for Nippur, 122, 18; 180, 11.

translation.original_ref: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest, section 2
translation.original_text:
E-anna, temple in Erech, 123, 30; 125; 148, 12; 213, 18.

E-babbar, temple of the sun god, 152; 158, 11; 166, 1. Perhaps read E-barra.

E-daranna, temple of Enki in Babylon, 169, 25; 170, 29. See BL. 133.

translation.original_ref: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest, section 3
translation.original_text:
_edelu_ = _ederu_, be gloomy, 216, 10.

_e-dub_, house of learning, 117, 39.

_e-gal_, palace, No. 19, Rev. 3; 115, 11; 131, 7; 134, 22; 158, 9.

Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest, section 5
translation.original_text:
Emah, Esmah, ritual house of the water cult of Marduk, 163, 7; 115, 4.

E-malga-sud, temple, 181, 24; 141, 3.

E-meteg, daughter of Ninkasi, 144.

translation.original_text:

E-namtila, temple, 160, 4; 169, 24.

_en-a-nu-un_, _en-a-nun_, title of Innini and Gula, 173, 2.

translation.original_ref: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest, section 7
translation.original_text:
Enbilulu, title of Marduk, 170, 5.

_EN-KA-KA, bel dababi_, 194, 2.

Enki, god. Hymn to, No. 20, 113, 7; 114, 10; 116, 21; 122, 7; 149, 16.
